title: Learning-Guided Decision Ordering for Scalable ATPG
abstract: We present a learning-guided decision-ordering strategy for automatic test pattern generation that steers PODEM backtracking using a graph model of the netlist. On industrial-scale benchmarks the method reaches equivalent stuck-at and transition coverage with substantially fewer patterns and lower runtime, without modifying the underlying completeness guarantees of the search.

title: Testability-Aware Partitioning for Heterogeneous Chiplet Systems
abstract: Disaggregating a system into chiplets improves yield and reuse but complicates test access. We formulate partitioning as a joint optimization of interconnect cost and known-good-die testability, and show that co-optimizing the two yields packages that are both cheaper to assemble and easier to diagnose.

title: A Reproducible Low-Power RTL Methodology on Open PDKs
venue: ACM Transactions on Design Automation of Electronic Systems (TODAES)
abstract: We describe a low-power RTL methodology built entirely on open process design kits and open EDA, enabling independent reproduction of power results. The methodology combines clock-gating discovery, activity-aware synthesis, and a reporting harness that makes power numbers auditable end to end.

title: Reusing Scan Infrastructure for Hardware Trojan Detection
venue: IEEE Symposium on Hardware Oriented Security and Trust (HOST)
abstract: Dedicated Trojan-detection hardware is expensive. We show that existing scan and compression structures already carry enough side-channel and structural signal to flag a broad class of hardware Trojans, letting trust verification ride on infrastructure the design already pays for.

title: SparseCore: A Reconfigurable Dataflow for Structured-Sparse Inference
abstract: We introduce a reconfigurable dataflow accelerator whose datapath skips zero work under structured sparsity. A lightweight compiler maps common model shapes onto the fabric, delivering strong energy efficiency without sacrificing generality across workloads.

title: Hierarchical Scan Compression for Billion-Gate Designs
abstract: As designs cross the billion-gate mark, flat scan compression stops scaling. We propose a hierarchical compression scheme that keeps test data volume and application time bounded while preserving diagnosis resolution across the hierarchy.

title: ML Congestion Prediction for Open-Source Physical Design
abstract: Routing congestion is expensive to discover late in the flow. We train a graph surrogate that predicts congestion hotspots from placement, letting an open-source flow re-partition before routing and cutting iteration count on representative designs.

title: Error-Resilient Encoding for Archival DNA Storage
venue: ACM Journal on Emerging Technologies in Computing (JETC)
abstract: Synthesis and sequencing introduce insertions and deletions that classical block codes handle poorly. We propose an encoding that trades a modest density penalty for strong resilience to realistic indel error profiles, recovering payloads that baseline schemes lose.
